originalTitle: Qantas delays non-stop flights from Sydney to London – again
summary: Problematic history of airline’s Project Sunrise continues, with scheduled start date pushed back to October 2027
content: Qantas has again delayed its planned non-stop service between Sydney and London, with the flights now scheduled to start in October 2027. But the airline noted on Wednesday that “aircraft delivery, on-sale and service commencement” were all “subject to regulatory approvals and aircraft certification”. Qantas previously blamed delays on an extended wait for delivery of new Airbus A350s designed specifically for the new ultra-long-haul flights from Australia’s east coast. Qantas announced Project Sunrise in 2017. The Covid-19 pandemic pushed back the airline’s predicted 2022 launch and, since then, the service has been repeatedly delayed. In November 2025, the company said the service would start in the first half of 2027, with Airbus set to deliver the first specially configured A350-1000ULR by late 2026. But Airbus earlier this month revealed the first aircraft would be delivered in April 2027, pushing back the start date yet again. The Qantas chief executive, Vanessa Hudson, said on Wednesday: “We made a commitment in 2017 that Qantas would conquer the final frontier of long-haul aviation and connect Australia’s east coast directly to London … From October 2027, that promise becomes reality.” Direct flights between Sydney and London were expected to be four hours shorter than typical one-stop flights, Qantas said. The airline is already flying Perth to London and Sydney to London via Singapore services. The A350-1000ULR is designed to fly 16,000km over 22 hours non-stop, utilising an extra 20,000 litre fuel tank. Qantas is buying 12 aircraft with 238 seats rather than the 300-plus seats on other A350-1000s. The first Project Sunrise Sydney to London tickets could go on sale in February 2027, the airline said on Wednesday. Qantas did not disclose prices. Qantas also plans a Sydney to New York non-stop route. The airline said it would reveal a launch date for the US direct flights sometime in 2027.
chineseTitle: 澳洲航空再次推迟从悉尼飞往伦敦的直飞航班
chineseSummary: 航空公司“日出计划”的问题历史仍在继续，计划启动日期推迟至 2027 年 10 月
chineseContent: 澳洲航空再次推迟了计划中的悉尼和伦敦之间的直飞航班，航班现定于 2027 年 10 月开始。但该航空公司周三指出，“飞机交付、销售和服务启动”均“需要获得监管部门的批准和飞机认证”。澳洲航空此前将航班延误归咎于专为澳大利亚东海岸新超远程航班设计的新型空客 A350 飞机交付等待时间过长。澳洲航空于 2017 年宣布推出“日出计划”。Covid-19 大流行推迟了该航空公司原定于 2022 年推出的服务，此后该服务一再推迟。 2025年11月，该公司表示该服务将于2027年上半年开始，空客将在2026年底前交付第一架特殊配置的A350-1000ULR。但空客本月早些时候透露，第一架飞机将于2027年4月交付，再次推迟了开始日期。澳洲航空首席执行官凡妮莎·哈德森 (Vanessa Hudson) 周三表示：“我们在 2017 年做出承诺，澳洲航空将征服长途航空的最后前沿，将澳大利亚东海岸直接连接到伦敦……从 2027 年 10 月开始，这一承诺将成为现实。”澳洲航空表示，悉尼和伦敦之间的直飞航班预计比典型的一站式航班短四个小时。该航空公司已开通珀斯经新加坡飞往伦敦以及悉尼经新加坡飞往伦敦的航班。 A350-1000ULR 设计可在 22 小时内不间断飞行 16,000 公里，使用额外的 20,000 升油箱。澳洲航空将购买 12 架拥有 238 个座位的飞机，而不是其他拥有 300 多个座位的 A350-1000 飞机。该航空公司周三表示，首张“Project Sunrise”悉尼至伦敦机票将于 2027 年 2 月开始销售。澳洲航空没有透露价格。澳洲航空也计划
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
